subject: Geography
title: Arctic Tundra
description: Learn Arctic Tundra in one minute.

hook.text: The Arctic tundra is a treeless landscape where the growing season lasts just 50 to 60 days.

buildup.text: Permafrost beneath the surface prevents deep roots—only mosses, lichens, and low shrubs survive.

discovery.text: In summer, the tundra explodes with wildflowers and millions of migrating birds.

twist.text: As the Arctic warms, shrubs and even trees are creeping northward—the tundra is greening.

climax.text: The treeline is moving—a landscape that defined the Arctic for millennia is transforming.

punchline.text: The tundra is turning into forest. No one knows where it stops.

quiz.question: Why are trees unable to grow on Arctic tundra?
options: There is no sunlight in summer
